name: Code Compilation via Visual Basic Command Line Compiler
description: Detects successful code compilation using the Visual Basic Command Line Compiler (vbc.exe). While vbc.exe is a legitimate tool, its use to compile code outside of standard development environments is uncommon in most enterprises and can indicate malicious activity, such as adversaries compiling malware to evade defenses. cvtres.exe is a resource file conversion tool that is often used in the compilation process with vbc.exe.

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: The timestamp of the process creation event.

parameters.name: event_id
parameters.description: The Sysmon Event ID for Process Creation (1).

parameters.name: process_name
parameters.description: The name of the process that was created, which should be 'cvtres.exe'.

parameters.name: parent_process_name
parameters.description: The name of the parent process, which should be 'vbc.exe'.

parameters.name: command_line
parameters.description: The full command line used to execute cvtres.exe.

parameters.name: user
parameters.description: The user account under which the process was executed.

parameters.name: host
parameters.description: The hostname where the event occurred.
